collector/quickstart/collector.purpose: Top-level Benthos runtime configuration for the quickstart collector. config.yaml is the minimal server bootstrap (HTTP management API on 4195, Prometheus metrics, optional OTel tracer stub) that Benthos loads first; the actual pipeline lives in streams/ (input+output) and shared infra in resources/ (the dedupe cache).

patterns.name: Layered config: server vs streams vs resources
patterns.description: config.yaml owns only server-level concerns (http.address, metrics, tracer); stream processors live in streams/ and shared caches in resources/ — never merge these into config.yaml.
patterns.example: config.yaml: http.address + metrics.prometheus; streams/input.yaml: processors; resources/dedupe-cache.yaml: cache declaration

patterns.name: Inproc channel couples the two streams
patterns.description: streams/input.yaml publishes to inproc: openmeter and streams/output.yaml reads from the same named channel; both names must stay in sync or events are silently dropped.
patterns.example:
input.yaml output: inproc: openmeter
output.yaml input: inproc: openmeter

patterns.name: Prometheus metrics always enabled, single backend
patterns.description: The top-level config always exposes metrics: prometheus: {}; any replacement must stay scrape-compatible and Benthos supports only one metrics sink.

patterns.name: OTel tracer is an opt-in commented stub
patterns.description: The tracer block is commented out; enabling it means uncommenting and setting address — do not add a second tracer block.

patterns.name: Secrets via ${ENV_VAR} interpolation
patterns.description: OPENMETER_URL and OPENMETER_TOKEN are referenced via ${ENV_VAR} in stream configs, never hardcoded.

key_file_guides.role: Benthos server bootstrap: HTTP management API (port 4195), Prometheus metrics, optional OTel tracer stub.
key_file_guides.watch_for: debug_endpoints: false in production (true exposes internal Benthos state); changing the HTTP address requires matching docker-compose port mappings.

key_file_guides.role: Declares the named in-memory dedupe_cache (1h TTL) used by stream processors for CloudEvent deduplication.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Renaming dedupe_cache breaks all cache: references in streams/input.yaml with no compile-time error — silent dedup bypass.

key_file_guides.role: HTTP CloudEvent receiver: Content-Type switch, xxhash64 dedup against dedupe_cache, schema validation, inproc publish to openmeter, sync HTTP response.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Removing the fan_out sync_response branch silences HTTP status codes; bypassing the Content-Type switch sends unnormalized bytes to dedup, corrupting deduplication.

key_file_guides.role: Inproc consumer: reads from openmeter channel, buffers to SQLite for durability, forwards batches to the OpenMeter API.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Renaming inproc: openmeter without matching input.yaml drops all events; removing the SQLite buffer loses events on restart.

anti_patterns: Adding stream processors or cache declarations directly in config.yaml — they belong in streams/ and resources/
Hardcoding OPENMETER_URL or OPENMETER_TOKEN instead of ${ENV_VAR} interpolation
Renaming the dedupe_cache label without updating all cache: references — silent dedup bypass with no error
Enabling debug_endpoints: true outside local development — exposes internal Benthos state
Adding a second metrics backend alongside prometheus — Benthos supports one metrics sink

decisions.decision: config.yaml is a minimal bootstrap; streams and resources load as separate files
decisions.rationale: Keeps the HTTP/metrics layer stable and independently reviewable while stream logic evolves separately.

decisions.decision: In-memory dedup cache with 1h TTL instead of Redis
decisions.rationale: The quickstart must run with zero external dependencies beyond the OpenMeter API; Redis would add a docker-compose service.

decisions.decision: Two-stream split (input + output) via a named inproc channel
decisions.rationale: Decouples HTTP ingestion latency from batch output delivery; the SQLite buffer in output gives durability across restarts without blocking the HTTP receiver.
